domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
In the context of a session, is it always association with at least one play, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A session can exist without any play.
2. A session must have at least one play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A session can exist without any play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A session must have at least one play.
questions.selected: Option 2: A session must have at least one play.

questions.question:
Should location be modeled as a separate class associated with Theater, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Location is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual location properties.
2. Location is modeled as a simple representation in Theater. This is suitable when location details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the location details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for location is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: Model location as a simple representation in Theater.

questions.question:
Should the location in theater be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use location as string attribute in theater, which allows any text value.
2. Use location as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: the northwest theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The location can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The location must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for location.

questions.question:
In the context of a theater, is it always associated with at least one play, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A theater can exist without any play.
2. A theater must have at least one play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A theater can exist without any play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A theater must have at least one play.
questions.selected: Option 1: A theater can exist without any play.

questions.question:
In the context of a play, is it always association with at least one theater, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A play can exist without any theater.
2. A play must have at least one theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A play can exist without any theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A play must have at least one theater.
questions.selected: Option 1: A play can exist without any theater.

questions.question:
In the context of a play, can there be more than one theater involved, or is it always associated with just one?
For example:
1. A play is linked to a single theater.
2. A play is linked to multiple theaters.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each play is always linked to a single theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A play can be linked to multiple theaters.
questions.selected: Option 1: Each play is always linked to a single theater.

questions.question:
When thinking about the domain, is person a relevant concept that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Person is a key concept in this domain and should be modeled.
2. Person is not relevant or seems like an error and should be removed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Person is relevant and should be kept.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Person is not relevant and should be removed.
questions.selected: Option 1: Person is relevant and should be kept.

questions.question:
The concept session potentially represents a link between plays and theaters. How should we understand this connection?

For example:
1. A session is a concept, but it does not represent a link between a play and a theater.
2. A session is a concept and a link that connects a play and a theater. 

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The session does not represent a link between a play and a theater.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The session is a concept and a link between a play and a theater.
questions.selected: Option 2: The session is a concept and a link between a play and a theater.

questions.question:
Thinking about person, and its kinds: participant or author. Is it possible to have a person that is not one of those specific types?

For example:
1. There are some people that are not part of the kinds participant or author.
2. Every person must be of a specific kind participant or author.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Some people exists that is not of the kind participant or author.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Every person is of a specific kind participant or author.
questions.selected: Option 2: Every person is of a specific kind participant or author.
